data.narrative:
NordSpace is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Rahul Goel that has raised $38M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (ClearSpace, Aalyria, Hubble Network, Sift) and 5 public Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The Stage-Progression method applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to next stage, using the $38M raised as a proxy base, yielding $76M. The Peer-Median method uses 4 direct private comparables with valuations ranging from $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria), anchored on a $400M median. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to unavailable revenue data for the target. The peer private valuation range spans $274M–$1.3B, with the median at $400M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $65M to $460M, with a median of $238M across the two applicable methods. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations (4.7x range) and the absence of revenue visibility, which precludes P/S-based anchoring to the public comp set. The Stage-Progression anchor at $76M represents a floor tied mechanically to capital raised; upside depends on operational performance and market position relative to the peer set.
